summary: Iteration 10 Complete - Dashboard UI theme update verified. Left sidebar, light theme, DM Sans font, explanative KPI cards all working correctly.

design_issues.screen: Other pages (Job Openings, Candidates, Interviews, Contacts)
issues: Still using dark theme - intentional per user request 'do not change unrelated modules'. Creates visual inconsistency between Home dashboard (light) and other pages (dark).

critical_code_review_comments: Visual inconsistency: Home dashboard uses light theme while other pages (Job Openings, Candidates, Interviews, Contacts) still use dark theme. This is intentional per user request but may confuse users.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data - used existing data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dashboard UI theme update complete. Key changes: 1) DashboardLayout.js has left sidebar (220px wide) with dark slate-900 bg, logo at top, nav links, profile at bottom. 2) index.css imports DM Sans font, sets #F1F5F9 background. 3) KPICard.js has colorMap for blue/green/red/purple/amber color variants with light backgrounds. 4) Home.js uses white bg cards with lighter borders. 5) StatusBadge.js has light theme status colors. Other pages (Job Openings, Candidates, Interviews, Contacts) still use dark theme - intentional per user request. Key data-testids: nav-home, nav-job-openings, nav-candidates, nav-interviews, nav-contacts, profile-icon-btn, logout-btn, kpi-active-candidates, kpi-shortlisted, kpi-rejected, vendor-filter, date-filters, conversion-metrics, drop-off-analysis, vendor-performance-table, vendor-row-0.

sidebar.profile: At bottom with user name and email
